items.title: Proxy for OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, use any LLM with those apps
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source proxy called OpenCodex lets you route your Claude Code and Codex terminal sessions through any LLM API, including cheaper models like DeepSeek or Mistral. This is a massive cost-saver if you are running heavy terminal-based coding sessions and want to avoid burning through expensive Anthropic credits. To try this today, clone the repo, run the local proxy server, and point your Claude Code configuration to your local endpoint to start saving on API costs.

items.title: Claude Security Plugin for Claude Code Now in Beta
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has launched a beta security plugin specifically for Claude Code to automatically scan and flag potential security vulnerabilities or exposed secrets before they get committed. Since you are using Claude Code to build AI-ingest and managing your first real GitHub repo with strict commit discipline, this keeps your Supabase keys and API secrets safe. You can try this today by running the update command for Claude Code and enabling the security plugin in your terminal settings.

items.title: Show HN: Promptrack A local menu bar app that tracks your Claude Code usage
items.why_it_matters: Promptrack is a new, lightweight macOS menu bar app designed specifically to monitor your Claude Code token usage and costs locally. It helps you keep an eye on your API spend in real-time without having to manually parse Anthropic's dashboard. You can download the app today, plug in your local log path, and watch your exact session costs update live as you build.

items.title: Rabbitty – a native Mac terminal for running AI coding agents in parallel
items.why_it_matters: Rabbitty is a native Mac terminal app designed to let you run multiple AI coding agents, like Claude Code, in parallel. If you find yourself waiting on long terminal generations while building AI-ingest, this lets you multitask by running a backend Supabase task in one agent while a Next.js frontend task runs in another. Download the latest release from GitHub today and spin up two concurrent Claude Code sessions to speed up your shipping workflow.

items.title: Show HN: Claude Code desktop alternative for macOS
items.why_it_matters: Skeezo is a new macOS desktop alternative for Claude Code, offering a visual interface instead of a pure terminal environment. If you want a dedicated workspace to direct Claude's keyboard work without cluttering your main terminal, this gives you a clean GUI to manage files and prompts. Download the app today and run your next AI-ingest feature build through its interface to see if a visual workspace improves your vibe-coding speed.
